description: Fix the PostgreSQL `IDatabaseFingerprintReader` so it reads the provisioned `dms."EffectiveSchema"` table using the exact identifier casing and quoting emitted by DDL provisioning. Acceptance: a correctly provisioned PostgreSQL database is not misclassified as unprovisioned; the existence check and singleton-row read both succeed against the real schema; requests only return "Database Not Provisioned" when the table or singleton row is actually missing; add coverage that exercises the reader against a provisioned PostgreSQL database or an equivalent end-to-end contract test.
steps-to-verify: Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql/PostgresqlDatabaseFingerprintReader.cs` to use the same table and column identifiers as the PostgreSQL provisioning/DDL path.
Add a test that provisions or simulates the real PostgreSQL schema shape and verifies `ReadFingerprintAsync()` returns the stored fingerprint instead of `null`.
Run the PostgreSQL reader contract/integration test added for this fix.

description: Fix the `DatabaseFingerprintProvider` fault-retry path so concurrent callers cannot evict a newer healthy cache entry after an earlier read fails. Acceptance: when the first fingerprint read faults and a later retry succeeds, subsequent callers observe the recovered cached value; concurrent awaiters of the same failed read do not remove a replacement entry created by a retry; the provider still performs a single read for concurrent first-use requests and still retries after genuine transient failures.
steps-to-verify: Update `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/DatabaseFingerprintProvider.cs` to remove faulted entries by key-and-instance identity instead of blindly removing by connection string.
Add a concurrency-focused unit test that reproduces: one cached read faults, one caller retries and succeeds, and a later catch from the original fault does not remove the recovered cache entry.

description: Remove duplicated runtime/provisioning fingerprint SQL definitions so table names, column names, and singleton-read conventions come from one source of truth per dialect. Acceptance: the PostgreSQL and MSSQL runtime fingerprint readers do not carry hand-maintained copies of identifier metadata that can drift from provisioning; changing the `EffectiveSchema` naming contract requires editing one shared definition per dialect; existing runtime validation behavior and provisioning behavior remain unchanged apart from the bug fix above.
steps-to-verify: Refactor the fingerprint reader query metadata so the runtime readers and provisioning code share the same dialect-specific `EffectiveSchema` identifier definitions where practical.
Add or update tests that would fail if runtime reader identifiers diverge from provisioning identifiers for either PostgreSQL or MSSQL.
